author: marcus.pittman
body: Section 4.3 on financial oversight needs to reference the deacon board approval threshold of $2,500. Can Gloria update that wording?

author: gloria.tate
body: Updated Section 4.3 with the $2,500 threshold language. Please review and confirm.

author: jwilkins
body: The quorum requirement listed here (4 members) conflicts with what was agreed at the January meeting. Should be 5 members present. Please correct.

author: gloria.tate
body: Corrected to 5 members. Version bumped. Thank you Pastor Wilkins.

author: lamar.boykin
body: Gulf Coast Roofing never sent the second bid. Should we note that only one bid was received or table the roof item to March?

author: marcus.pittman
body: Table to March meeting. I will follow up with Gulf Coast Roofing this week.

author: theresa.nguyen
body: The Magnolia Painting Co. quote listed here is $1,200 but the invoice I have on file shows $1,450. Which figure is correct for the record?

author: gloria.tate
body: Confirmed with Calvin — the correct figure is $1,450. I have updated the minutes. Sorry for the typo.

author: lamar.boykin
body: Added the April 3 HVAC filter replacement entry. Technician was Jorge Salinas from Biloxi HVAC Services. Took about 45 minutes, no issues.

author: theresa.nguyen
body: Should we also log the kitchen exhaust hood cleaning that happened the same day? I was not sure if that belongs here or in the Kitchen Equipment Inventory page.

author: lamar.boykin
body: Theresa — log it here. The maintenance log is the catch-all. I will add it now.

author: calvin.hargrove
body: Gulf Janitorial Supply changed their phone number. New number is 228-555-0411. Updating the page now.

author: marcus.pittman
body: Also need to add a contact for the fire extinguisher inspection company we used in October. I will send Calvin the details.

author: lamar.boykin
body: Step 6 says to order paint two weeks out but last year we ran short. Suggest changing to three weeks lead time so we can get a second order in if needed.

author: theresa.nguyen
body: Agree with Lamar. Three weeks also gives time to confirm volunteer count before finalizing the order quantity.

author: marcus.pittman
body: The miscellaneous line at $2,300 seems low given the fellowship hall flooring estimate we received. Should we request a budget amendment before April?

author: jwilkins
body: Agreed. Let us bring a budget amendment request to the February committee meeting. Gloria please add this to the agenda.

author: calvin.hargrove
body: The exit sign in the back hallway near the nursery failed the inspection. I have ordered a replacement bulb kit. Should be installed by end of week.

author: lamar.boykin
body: Exit sign fixed May 22. I updated the checklist item to show it passed re-inspection.

author: jwilkins
body: Deacon Pittman, can you clarify the status of the roof drainage claim mentioned in section 3? I did not see any correspondence from the insurance carrier on that.

author: marcus.pittman
body: Pastor, the carrier replied December 9 and denied the claim as wear and tear. I am drafting an appeal. Will post the update once it is submitted.

author: theresa.nguyen
body: The landscaping contract renewal quote from Coastal is 8% higher than last year. Do we want to get a competing bid before April or accept the renewal?

author: lamar.boykin
body: Let us get one competing bid. I can reach out to Gulf South Grounds this week and have something back by March 10.
